member.name: Ms Julie Minns
member.synopsis: Ms Julie Minns is the Labour MP for Carlisle , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Neurological Diseases: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps his Department is taking to establish a targeted, ambitious and measurable Modern Service Framework for neurological conditions.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to reducing avoidable emergency admissions for people with neurological conditions by improving earlier diagnosis, strengthening community-based care, and ensuring better coordination between services.NHS England is encouraging...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to reducing avoidable emergency admissions for people with neurological conditions by improving earlier diagnosis, strengthening community-based care, and ensuring better coordination between services.NHS England is encouraging...

writtenQuestions.heading: Electronic Cigarettes: Cybersecurity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment his Department has made of the potential security and data privacy impact of so-called smart vapes and other internet-connected vaping devices.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department is aware of the increasing role of technology in vapes. That is why the Tobacco and Vapes Act includes broad powers to regulate the technological features of vapes and associated software. In October 2025, we launched a call for evidence to...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Electric Bicycles: Accidents
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the levels of claims to the The Motor Insurers’ Bureau from victims of accidents caused by e-bikes with an electric motor capable of a continuous rated power above 250
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made a specific assessment of the levels of claims to The Motor Insurers’ Bureau from victims of collisions caused by e-bikes that do not comply with legal limits on power and speed. The Department is working with the Motor Insurer’...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Family Hubs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to roll out Best Start Family Hubs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On April 1 2026, we announced that Best Start Family Hubs in newly funded areas are open to families, as the government delivers on its pledge to ensure there is a hub in every local authority and building towards our ambition of having have 1,000 hubs an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: Carlisle
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the closure of applications for Gigabit Broadband Vouchers in Cumbria on households in Carlisle constituency who do not have a fi
writtenQuestions.answerText: Based on Ofcom’s Connected Nations reporting for gigabit coverage in the constituency of Carlisle, as of January 2026, 91% of premises had access to gigabit-capable broadband, which is a significant increase from 69% in January 2024 and is above the UK gi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: Carlisle
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps her Department is taking to deliver fibre-optic broadband to properties in Carlisle constituency that have not been included in the Project Gigabit roll-out to date.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Based on Ofcom’s Connected Nations reporting for gigabit coverage in the constituency of Carlisle, as of January 2026, 91% of premises had access to gigabit-capable broadband, which is a significant increase from 69% in January 2024 and is above the UK gi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Affordable Housing: Almshouses and Charities

writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the answer given to Question UIN 123092 on 31 March 2026.

writtenQuestions.heading: Solicitors Regulation Authority
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many interventions have there been by the Solicitors Regulation Authority into law firms involving (a) the sudden or disorderly closure of a firm, (b) the loss or misappropriation of client funds and (c) the
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the disruption and difficulty that the collapse of a law firm has both for employees and its clients. The legal profession in England and Wales, together with its regulators, operates independently of government. The Solicitor Re...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Individual Savings Accounts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the Answer of 28 January 2026 to Question 109300 on Individual Savings Accounts, whether existing Lifetime ISA holders will be permitted to transfer their savings without penalty into the new product tha
writtenQuestions.answerText: At Autumn Budget 2025, the Government announced that it will consult in early 2026 on introducing a new, simpler ISA product for first time buyers. The new ISA product will be offered in place of the Lifetime ISA. The consultation will consider how existi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Gender Based Violence: Men
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps her Department is taking to ensure that the commitments in the Men and Boys Explanatory Note to the Government’s Freedom from Violence and Abuse Strategy are implemented by police forces, p
writtenQuestions.answerText: The ‘Men and Boys Explanatory Note’ explains how the Strategy considers and addresses the needs of men and boys in detail. It underlines how the strategy speaks to men and boys, by addressing the key issues that the male victims’ sector has raised with us...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Mobile Phones: Standards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps her Department is taking to help improve the resilience of mobile networks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Reliable access to mobile services is essential. We recognise the impact that power cuts and storms can have on services, especially in rural areas – Storm Goretti is a recent example of this. Ofcom is analysing power backup requirements to ensure adequat...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Waste Disposal: Scotland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps his Department is taking to help prevent Scottish biodegradable municipal waste from being shipped to (a) English border constituencies and (b) other English constituencies,
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the hon. Member to the reply given to the Rt. Hon. Member for Aldridge-Brownhills, Wendy Morton, on 11 July 2025, PQ UIN 65784.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Armed Forces: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what progress he has made on the rollout of Programme Cortisone.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Programme CORTISONE will provide the Defence Medical Services (DMS) with an up-to-date electronic healthcare information management system to administer the healthcare of the Defence population to maximise fitness for role. The delivery of Programme CORTI...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence
